content.problemStatement:
The Chef gives you a singly linked list AAA integers and ask you to help him reverse the list.

Complete the function "listReverse" in the code snippet that takes a single argument: head of the linked list.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer NNN - representing the number of elements of the linked list.
The second line contains NNN integers - representing the elements of the linked list.


Output Format
For each testcase, output will be in a single line containing a list returned by the function listReverse.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (the number of nodes in the linked list). The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the node values in order from head to tail.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the values of the reversed linked list, space‑separated.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: list 1→2→3→4→5 reversed is 5→4→3→2→1. Second test case: list 1→1→3→2→1 reversed is 1→2→3→1→1.
Reversing the three‑element list yields the values in opposite order.

hidden.label: edge case with negatives and zeros

solution.approach:
For each test case we read the values into an array (or vector). Reversing a singly linked list can be done in O(N) time by iterating once and changing the next pointers, but since we only need to output the reversed order, we can simply output the array from the last element to the first. This avoids explicit pointer manipulation and works within the same time and space bounds. The algorithm:
1. Read N and the N values.
2. Store the values in a container.
3. Iterate the container backwards and print the values separated by a space.
The total time per test case is O(N) and the extra space used is O(N) for storing the values.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the linked list.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the array that holds the node values.
